task_completion.description: Open the Task Tags settings for the LegacyAuth project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Eclipse Properties dialog for the 'LegacyAuth' project is open, specifically showing the 'Task Tags' section under 'Java Compiler'.

task_completion.description: Configure the 'SECURITY' task tag with High priority.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Task Tags settings, 'SECURITY' is present in the list of tags and its priority is set to 'High'.

task_completion.description: Configure the 'PERF' task tag with Low priority.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Task Tags settings, 'PERF' is present in the list of tags and its priority is set to 'Low'.
The file 'UserDAO.java' is open in the Eclipse editor.

task_completion.description: Remove the hardcoded backdoor vulnerability from UserDAO.java.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Eclipse editor for 'UserDAO.java', the code block containing `if ("superuser".equals(username) && "pass123".equals(password))` (originally around line 76) is no longer present.

integrity.description: The agent used the Eclipse GUI to configure the task tags.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Eclipse Properties dialog being used, rather than a terminal or text editor modifying the `.settings/org.eclipse.jdt.core.prefs` file directly.

integrity.description: The agent edited the Java file using the Eclipse IDE.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show 'UserDAO.java' being edited within the Eclipse IDE, not in a terminal editor like nano or vim.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task is performed in Eclipse on the 'LegacyAuth' project. The custom task tags to configure are 'SECURITY' (High priority) and 'PERF' (Low priority). The target file is 'src/main/java/com/legacy/auth/UserDAO.java'. The backdoor code to remove is `if ("superuser".equals(username) && "pass123".equals(password))` located around line 76.
